risk_interpretation: Terminated users had active accounts, late access removal, missing removal dates, or login after termination.
recommendation: Automate HR-to-IT deprovisioning alerts and create a weekly terminated-user access exception report.

risk_interpretation: Active privileged accounts did not have MFA enabled, including 14 System Administrators.
recommendation: Require MFA before privileged access is granted and review privileged-MFA exceptions weekly.

risk_interpretation: Access was granted before the approval date, meaning the approval control did not operate before provisioning.
recommendation: Configure workflow controls to block provisioning until approval is completed.

risk_interpretation: Admin or elevated access was granted before approval, increasing unauthorized access risk.
recommendation: Prioritize admin/elevated access requests for approval enforcement and exception review.

risk_interpretation: Approved and provisioned access requests lacked documented business justification.
recommendation: Require business justification as a mandatory field before approval or provisioning.

risk_interpretation: Admin/elevated access was approved or provisioned without documented business need.
recommendation: Require stronger evidence for admin/elevated access and review these requests monthly.

risk_interpretation: Access request and MFA tickets had the highest SLA breach rates.
recommendation: Review routing, staffing, escalation rules, and knowledge base quality for access and MFA tickets.

risk_interpretation: Repeated reopening suggests incomplete first-contact resolution or unclear user instructions.
recommendation: Improve knowledge base articles and first-contact resolution scripts for account lockout, MFA, and password reset issues.

risk_interpretation: High escalation suggests Tier 1 may not have enough access, guidance, or routing clarity.
recommendation: Review escalation criteria and create clearer routing rules for access request, MFA, and account lockout tickets.
